pattern: Split the required change at every position into how much it must go up and how much it must go down, then each side becomes an independent skyline that costs one operation per fresh rise in height.
hints: Look at the per-position difference between target and nums; a range +1 only ever helps where that difference is positive, and a range -1 only ever helps where it is negative, so the two directions never need to share an operation.

Treat the positive differences as a skyline (heights along the array) and separately the negative differences (as positive heights) as a second skyline - a contiguous range operation is exactly one unit of 'wall' in one of these skylines.

The minimum number of unit-height, variable-width rectangles needed to build a skyline is the sum of its positive increases from one position to the next (treating both ends as height zero); add that quantity for both skylines.

edge_inputs.description: every position only ever needs to increase
a mix of increases and decreases across the array
already equal, so nothing to do
a single element just needs its own flat difference covered once
a tall spike in the middle should not force extra operations on its flat neighbors
an oscillating required direction prevents any single range operation from covering more than one position

stress.description: near-max length with random targets in both directions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = [rng.randint(1, 100000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, target]

stress.description: a jagged near-max sequence with many alternating small ups and downs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = []
    target = []
    v = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    for _ in range(n):
        nums.append(v)
        v = max(1, v + rng.randint(-5, 5))
        target.append(v)
    return [nums, target]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minimumOperations(self, nums, target):
        n = len(nums)
        pos_prev = 0
        neg_prev = 0
        total = 0
        for i in range(n):
            d = target[i] - nums[i]
            p = max(d, 0)
            ng = max(-d, 0)
            total += max(0, p - pos_prev)
            total += max(0, ng - neg_prev)
            pos_prev = p
            neg_prev = ng
        return total
